_meta.description: HITL approved/rejected/cancelled + shared-state-read-write fixtures migrated from d5-all.json (main additions from PR #4973 et al). These are D5-level fixtures without context scoping. TODO: distribute into per-integration d6/<slug>/ files with appropriate match.context keys.

response.content: Great choices! I will proceed with executing the selected steps for your trip to Mars. Let me work through each one.
No problem — I will not execute the Mars trip plan.

match.userMessage: Book a 30-minute onboarding call for Alice

response.content: Denied — Alice's onboarding call was not booked because no time slot was selected.

fixtures._comment: shared-state-read-write edited-name probe. The harness sets name=D5-PREF-PROBE before clicking Greet me; matching this proves changed preferences reached the model-visible system prompt instead of serving the default fixture.

response.content: Hi D5-PREF-PROBE - I can see your edited shared-state preferences in the current turn.

fixtures._comment: hitl-in-app — refund pill (#12345), approved branch. The .NET runtime suffixes the request_user_approval tool result id from the actual frontend result so approve/reject matching is deterministic without sequenceIndex.
